libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker service or external WebDAV server is required for meaningful coverage. Prefer in-memory XML/property/method tests and lightweight mocks or stubs for HttpServletRequest/HttpServletResponse when exercising servlet-facing APIs. No special environment variables or system properties were found to be required.

deterministic_setup.reason:
The artifact's public classes such as AbstractWebdavServlet, DavServletRequest, DavServletResponse, InputContextImpl, OutputContextImpl, and WebdavRequestImpl reference javax.servlet/http types, while jackrabbit-webdav declares servlet-api with provided scope so it is not supplied transitively to te
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8851 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The current native-image failure appears to be in existing locator tests rather than missing external setup, so local Gradle and Native Image verification may still require test or metadata fixes after adding the servlet API dependency.
SLF4J has no binding unless tests add one, but this is normally only a logging/no-op warning concern and not required for meaningful coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jackrabbit WebDAV exposes servlet-based public APIs and declares javax.servlet only as a provided dependency, so generated tests need an explicit servlet API test dependency to compile and exercise meaningful server/request/response coverage.
